member.name: T:MystenLabs.Sui.Grpc.SuiGrpcClient
member.summary: Sui gRPC client. Connects to a Sui full node gRPC endpoint (recommended over JSON-RPC for new integrations). Default endpoints: mainnet fullnode.mainnet.sui.io:443, testnet fullnode.testnet.sui.io:443, devnet fullnode.devnet.sui.io:443.

member.name: F:MystenLabs.Sui.Grpc.SuiGrpcClient.DefaultMainnetAddress
member.summary: Default mainnet gRPC address.

member.name: F:MystenLabs.Sui.Grpc.SuiGrpcClient.DefaultTestnetAddress
member.summary: Default testnet gRPC address.

member.name: F:MystenLabs.Sui.Grpc.SuiGrpcClient.DefaultDevnetAddress
member.summary: Default devnet gRPC address.

member.name: F:MystenLabs.Sui.Grpc.SuiGrpcClient.DefaultLocalAddress
member.summary: Default local full node gRPC address.

member.name: P:MystenLabs.Sui.Grpc.SuiGrpcClient.State
member.summary: State service (list objects, get balance, list balances, list dynamic fields, get coin info).

member.name: P:MystenLabs.Sui.Grpc.SuiGrpcClient.Ledger
member.summary: Ledger service (checkpoints, transaction blocks, service info).

member.name: P:MystenLabs.Sui.Grpc.SuiGrpcClient.TransactionExecution
member.summary: Transaction execution service (execute transaction block, dry run, etc.).

member.name: M:MystenLabs.Sui.Grpc.SuiGrpcClient.#ctor(System.String,System.Net.Http.HttpMessageHandler)
member.summary: Creates a gRPC client for the given address.
member.param: Base address (e.g. https://fullnode.mainnet.sui.io:443).
Optional HTTP handler (e.g. for auth or custom options).

member.name: M:MystenLabs.Sui.Grpc.SuiGrpcClient.#ctor(Grpc.Net.Client.GrpcChannel)
member.summary: Creates a gRPC client using an existing channel (e.g. for sharing or custom configuration).
member.param: Shared or pre-configured channel.

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.ErrorDetailsReflection
member.summary: Holder for reflection information generated from google/rpc/error_details.proto

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.ErrorDetailsReflection.Descriptor
member.summary: File descriptor for google/rpc/error_details.proto

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.ErrorInfo
member.summary:
Describes the cause of the error with structured details.
Example of an error when contacting the "pubsub.googleapis.com" API when it is not enabled:
```text,json { "reason": "API_DISABLED" "domain": "googleapis.com" "metadata": { "resource": "projects/123", "service": "pubsub.googleapis.com" } } ```
This response indicates that the pubsub.googleapis.com API is not enabled.
Example of an error that is returned when attempting to create a Spanner instance in a region that is out of stock:
```text,json { "reason": "STOCKOUT" "domain": "spanner.googleapis.com", "metadata": { "availableRegions": "us-central1,us-east2" } } ```

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.ErrorInfo.ReasonFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "reason" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.ErrorInfo.Reason
member.summary: The reason of the error. This is a constant value that identifies the proximate cause of the error. Error reasons are unique within a particular domain of errors. This should be at most 63 characters and match a regular expression of `[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]+[A-Z0-9]`, which represents UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.ErrorInfo.DomainFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "domain" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.ErrorInfo.Domain
member.summary: The logical grouping to which the "reason" belongs. The error domain is typically the registered service name of the tool or product that generates the error. Example: "pubsub.googleapis.com". If the error is generated by some common infrastructure, the error domain must be a globally unique value that identifies the infrastructure. For Google API infrastructure, the error domain is "googleapis.com".

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.ErrorInfo.MetadataFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "metadata" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.ErrorInfo.Metadata
member.summary:
Additional structured details about this error.
Keys must match a regular expression of `[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9-_]+` but should ideally be lowerCamelCase. Also, they must be limited to 64 characters in length. When identifying the current value of an exceeded limit, the units should be contained in the key, not the value. For example, rather than `{"instanceLimit": "100/request"}`, should be returned as, `{"instanceLimitPerRequest": "100"}`, if the client exceeds the number of instances that can be created in a single (batch) request.

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.RetryInfo
member.summary:
Describes when the clients can retry a failed request. Clients could ignore the recommendation here or retry when this information is missing from error responses.
It's always recommended that clients should use exponential backoff when retrying.
Clients should wait until `retry_delay` amount of time has passed since receiving the error response before retrying. If retrying requests also fail, clients should use an exponential backoff scheme to gradually increase the delay between retries based on `retry_delay`, until either a maximum number of retries have been reached or a maximum retry delay cap has been reached.

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.RetryInfo.RetryDelayFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "retry_delay" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.RetryInfo.RetryDelay
member.summary: Clients should wait at least this long between retrying the same request.

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.QuotaFailure
member.summary:
Describes how a quota check failed.
For example if a daily limit was exceeded for the calling project, a service could respond with a QuotaFailure detail containing the project id and the description of the quota limit that was exceeded. If the calling project hasn't enabled the service in the developer console, then a service could respond with the project id and set `service_disabled` to true.
Also see RetryInfo and Help types for other details about handling a quota failure.

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.QuotaFailure.ViolationsFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "violations" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.QuotaFailure.Violations
member.summary: Describes all quota violations.

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.QuotaFailure.Types
member.summary: Container for nested types declared in the QuotaFailure message type.

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.QuotaFailure.Types.Violation
member.summary: A message type used to describe a single quota violation. For example, a daily quota or a custom quota that was exceeded.

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.QuotaFailure.Types.Violation.SubjectFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "subject" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.QuotaFailure.Types.Violation.Subject
member.summary: The subject on which the quota check failed. For example, `clientip:<ip address of client>` or `project:<Google developer project id>`.

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.QuotaFailure.Types.Violation.DescriptionFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "description" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.QuotaFailure.Types.Violation.Description
member.summary:
A description of how the quota check failed. Clients can use this description to find more about the quota configuration in the service's public documentation, or find the relevant quota limit to adjust through developer console.
For example: "Service disabled" or "Daily Limit for read operations exceeded".

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure
member.summary:
Describes what preconditions have failed.
For example, if an RPC failed because it required the Terms of Service to be acknowledged, it could list the terms of service violation in the PreconditionFailure message.

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure.ViolationsFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "violations" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure.Violations
member.summary: Describes all precondition violations.

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure.Types
member.summary: Container for nested types declared in the PreconditionFailure message type.

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure.Types.Violation
member.summary: A message type used to describe a single precondition failure.

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure.Types.Violation.TypeFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "type" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure.Types.Violation.Type
member.summary: The type of PreconditionFailure. We recommend using a service-specific enum type to define the supported precondition violation subjects. For example, "TOS" for "Terms of Service violation".

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure.Types.Violation.SubjectFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "subject" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure.Types.Violation.Subject
member.summary: The subject, relative to the type, that failed. For example, "google.com/cloud" relative to the "TOS" type would indicate which terms of service is being referenced.

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure.Types.Violation.DescriptionFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "description" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.PreconditionFailure.Types.Violation.Description
member.summary:
A description of how the precondition failed. Developers can use this description to understand how to fix the failure.
For example: "Terms of service not accepted".

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.StatusReflection
member.summary: Holder for reflection information generated from google/rpc/status.proto

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.StatusReflection.Descriptor
member.summary: File descriptor for google/rpc/status.proto

member.name: T:Google.Rpc.Status
member.summary:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.
You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.Status.CodeFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "code" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.Status.Code
member.summary: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.Status.MessageFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "message" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.Status.Message
member.summary: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

member.name: F:Google.Rpc.Status.DetailsFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "details" field.

member.name: P:Google.Rpc.Status.Details
member.summary: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is a common set of message types for APIs to use.

Set of objects either referenced as inputs or produced as outputs by transactions included in this checkpoint.

member.summary: File descriptor for sui/rpc/v2/checkpoint_summary.proto

member.name: T:Sui.Rpc.V2.CheckpointSummary
member.summary:
A header for a checkpoint on the Sui blockchain.
On the Sui network, checkpoints define the history of the blockchain. They are quite similar to the concept of blocks used by other blockchains like Bitcoin or Ethereum. The Sui blockchain, however, forms checkpoints after transaction execution has already happened to provide a certified history of the chain, instead of being formed before execution.
Checkpoints commit to a variety of state, including but not limited to: - The hash of the previous checkpoint. - The set of transaction digests, their corresponding effects digests, as well as the set of user signatures that authorized its execution. - The objects produced by a transaction. - The set of live objects that make up the current state of the chain. - On epoch transitions, the next validator committee.
`CheckpointSummary`s themselves don't directly include all of the previous information but they are the top-level type by which all the information is committed to transitively via cryptographic hashes included in the summary. `CheckpointSummary`s are signed and certified by a quorum of the validator committee in a given epoch to allow verification of the chain's state.

member.summary: `CheckpointSummary` is not an evolvable structure - it must be readable by any version of the code. Therefore, to allow extensions to be added to `CheckpointSummary`, opaque data can be added to checkpoints, which can be deserialized based on the current protocol version.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.TransactionEffects.UnchangedConsensusObjects
member.summary: Consensus objects that are not mutated in this transaction. Unlike owned objects, read-only consensus objects' version are not committed in the transaction, and in order for a node to catch up and execute it without consensus sequencing, the version needs to be committed in the effects.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.TransactionEffects.AuxiliaryDataDigest
member.summary: Auxiliary data that are not protocol-critical, generated as part of the effects but are stored separately. Storing it separately allows us to avoid bloating the effects with data that are not critical. It also provides more flexibility on the format and type of the data.

member.summary: Container for nested types declared in the CommandArgumentError message type.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.TypeMismatch
member.summary: The type of the value does not match the expected type.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidBcsBytes
member.summary: The argument cannot be deserialized into a value of the specified type.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidUsageOfPureArgument
member.summary: The argument cannot be instantiated from raw bytes.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidArgumentToPrivateEntryFunction
member.summary: Invalid argument to private entry function. Private entry functions cannot take arguments from other Move functions.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.IndexOutOfBounds
member.summary:
Out of bounds access to input or results.
`index` field will be set indicating the invalid index value.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.SecondaryIndexOutOfBounds
member.summary:
Out of bounds access to subresult.
`index` and `subresult` fields will be set indicating the invalid index value.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidResultArity
member.summary: Invalid usage of result. Expected a single result but found either no return value or multiple. `index` field will be set indicating the invalid index value.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidGasCoinUsage
member.summary: Invalid usage of gas coin. The gas coin can only be used by-value with a `TransferObject` command.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidValueUsage
member.summary: Invalid usage of Move value. - Mutably borrowed values require unique usage. - Immutably borrowed values cannot be taken or borrowed mutably. - Taken values cannot be used again.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidObjectByValue
member.summary: Immutable objects cannot be passed by-value.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidObjectByMutRef
member.summary: Immutable objects cannot be passed by mutable reference, `&mut`.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.ConsensusObjectOperationNotAllowed
member.summary: Consensus object operations such as wrapping, freezing, or converting to owned are not allowed.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidArgumentArity
member.summary: Invalid argument arity. Expected a single argument but found a result that expanded to multiple arguments.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidTransferObject
member.summary: Object passed to TransferObject does not have public transfer, i.e. the `store` ability

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidMakeMoveVecNonObjectArgument
member.summary: First argument to MakeMoveVec is not an object. If no type is specified for MakeMoveVec, all arguments must be the same object type.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.ArgumentWithoutValue
member.summary: Specified argument location does not have a value and cannot be used

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.CannotMoveBorrowedValue
member.summary: Cannot move a borrowed value. The value's type does resulted in this argument usage being inferred as a move. This is likely due to the type not having the `copy` ability; although in rare cases, it could also be this is the last usage of a value without the `drop` ability.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.CannotWriteToExtendedReference
member.summary: Cannot write to an argument location that is still borrowed, and where that borrow is an extension of that reference. This is likely due to this argument being used in a Move call that returns a reference, and that reference is used in a later command.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.CommandArgumentError.Types.CommandArgumentErrorKind.InvalidReferenceArgument
member.summary: The argument specified cannot be used as a reference argument in the Move call. Either the argument is a mutable reference and it conflicts with another argument to the call, or the argument is mutable and another reference extends it and will be used in a later command.

member.summary:
The PackageId of this package
A package's `storage_id` is the Sui ObjectId of the package on-chain. Outside of system packages the `storage_id` for every package version is different.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.Package.OriginalId
member.summary:
The PackageId of the first published version of this package.
A package's `original_id` (sometimes also called its `runtime_id`) is the `storage_id` of the first version of this package that has been published. The `original_id`/`runtime_id` is stable across all versions of the package and does not ever change.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.NameRecord.RegistrationNftId
member.summary:
The ID of the `RegistrationNFT` assigned to this record.
The owner of the corresponding `RegistrationNFT` has the rights to be able to change and adjust the `target_address` of this domain.
It is possible that the ID changes if the record expires and is purchased by someone else.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.NameRecord.ExpirationTimestamp
member.summary:
Timestamp when the record expires.
This is either the expiration of the record itself or the expiration of this record's parent if this is a leaf record.

member.summary: This field will be populated with information about this coin type's `0x2::coin::CoinMetadata` if it exists and has not been wrapped.

member.summary: Field number for the "treasury" field.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.GetCoinInfoResponse.Treasury

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.GetCoinInfoResponse.RegulatedMetadataFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "regulated_metadata" field.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.GetCoinInfoResponse.RegulatedMetadata
member.summary:
If this coin type is a regulated coin, this field will be populated with information either from its Currency object in the CoinRegistry, or from its `0x2::coin::RegulatedCoinMetadata` object for coins that have not been migrated to the CoinRegistry
If this coin is not known to be regulated, only the coin_regulated_state field will be populated.

member.summary: Mask specifying which fields to read. If no mask is specified, defaults to `object_id,version,object_type`.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.ListOwnedObjectsRequest.ObjectTypeFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "object_type" field.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.ListOwnedObjectsRequest.ObjectType
member.summary:
Optional type filter to limit the types of objects listed.
Providing an object type with no type params will return objects of that type with any type parameter, e.g. `0x2::coin::Coin` will return all `Coin<T>` objects regardless of the type parameter `T`. Providing a type with a type param will restrict the returned objects to only those objects that match the provided type parameters, e.g. `0x2::coin::Coin<0x2::sui::SUI>` will only return `Coin<SUI>` objects.

member.name: M:Sui.Rpc.V2.SubscriptionService.SubscriptionServiceClient.SubscribeCheckpoints(Sui.Rpc.V2.SubscribeCheckpointsRequest,Grpc.Core.Metadata,System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Subscribe to the stream of checkpoints.
This API provides a subscription to the checkpoint stream for the Sui blockchain. When a subscription is initialized the stream will begin with the latest executed checkpoint as seen by the server. Responses are guaranteed to return checkpoints in-order and without gaps. This enables clients to know exactly the last checkpoint they have processed and in the event the subscription terminates (either by the client/server or by the connection breaking), clients will be able to reinitialize a subscription and then leverage other APIs in order to request data for the checkpoints they missed.
member.param: The request to send to the server.
The initial metadata to send with the call. This parameter is optional.
An optional deadline for the call. The call will be cancelled if deadline is hit.
An optional token for canceling the call.

member.name: M:Sui.Rpc.V2.SubscriptionService.SubscriptionServiceClient.SubscribeCheckpoints(Sui.Rpc.V2.SubscribeCheckpointsRequest,Grpc.Core.CallOptions)
member.summary:
Subscribe to the stream of checkpoints.
This API provides a subscription to the checkpoint stream for the Sui blockchain. When a subscription is initialized the stream will begin with the latest executed checkpoint as seen by the server. Responses are guaranteed to return checkpoints in-order and without gaps. This enables clients to know exactly the last checkpoint they have processed and in the event the subscription terminates (either by the client/server or by the connection breaking), clients will be able to reinitialize a subscription and then leverage other APIs in order to request data for the checkpoints they missed.
member.param: The request to send to the server.
The options for the call.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.SystemState.ValidatorReportRecords
member.summary:
A list of the records of validator reporting each other.
There is an entry in this list for each validator that has been reported at least once. Each record contains all the validators that reported them. If a validator has never been reported they don't have a record in this list. This lists persists across epoch: a peer continues being in a reported state until the reporter doesn't explicitly remove their report.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.SystemState.SafeModeFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "safe_mode" field.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.SystemState.SafeMode
member.summary: Whether the system is running in a downgraded safe mode due to a non-recoverable bug. This is set whenever we failed to execute advance_epoch, and ended up executing advance_epoch_safe_mode. It can be reset once we are able to successfully execute advance_epoch. The rest of the fields starting with `safe_mode_` are accumulated during safe mode when advance_epoch_safe_mode is executed. They will eventually be processed once we are out of safe mode.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.StorageFund.TotalObjectStorageRebates
member.summary: This is the sum of `storage_rebate` of all objects currently stored on-chain. To maintain this invariant, the only inflow of this balance is storage charges collected from transactions, and the only outflow is storage rebates of transactions, including both the portion refunded to the transaction senders as well as the non-refundable portion taken out and put into `non_refundable_balance`.

member.summary:
List of new validator candidates added during the current epoch. They will be processed at the end of the epoch.
key: u64 (index), value: 0x3::validator::Validator

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.ValidatorSet.PendingRemovalsFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "pending_removals" field.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.ValidatorSet.PendingRemovals
member.summary: Removal requests from the validators. Each element is an index pointing to `active_validators`.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.ValidatorSet.StakingPoolMappingsFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "staking_pool_mappings" field.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.ValidatorSet.StakingPoolMappings
member.summary:
Mappings from staking pool's ID to the sui address of a validator.
key: address (staking pool Id), value: address (sui address of the validator)

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.ValidatorSet.InactiveValidatorsFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "inactive_validators" field.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.ValidatorSet.InactiveValidators
member.summary:
Mapping from a staking pool ID to the inactive validator that has that pool as its staking pool. When a validator is deactivated the validator is removed from `active_validators` it is added to this table so that stakers can continue to withdraw their stake from it.
key: address (staking pool Id), value: 0x3::validator_wrapper::ValidatorWrapper

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.ValidatorSet.ValidatorCandidatesFieldNumber
member.summary: Field number for the "validator_candidates" field.

member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.ValidatorSet.ValidatorCandidates
member.summary:
Table storing preactive/candidate validators, mapping their addresses to their `Validator ` structs. When an address calls `request_add_validator_candidate`, they get added to this table and become a preactive validator. When the candidate has met the min stake requirement, they can call `request_add_validator` to officially add them to the active validator set `active_validators` next epoch.
key: address (sui address of the validator), value: 0x3::validator_wrapper::ValidatorWrapper

The entries start from the `activation_epoch` of this pool and contains exchange rates at the beginning of each epoch, i.e., right after the rewards for the previous epoch have been deposited into the pool.

member.name: T:Sui.Rpc.V2.TransactionExpiration.Types

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.TransactionExpiration.Types.TransactionExpirationKind.None
member.summary: The transaction has no expiration.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.TransactionExpiration.Types.TransactionExpirationKind.Epoch
member.summary: Validators won't sign and execute transaction unless the expiration epoch is greater than or equal to the current epoch.

member.name: F:Sui.Rpc.V2.TransactionExpiration.Types.TransactionExpirationKind.ValidDuring
member.summary:
This variant enables gas payments from address balances.
When transactions use address balances for gas payment instead of explicit gas coins, we lose the natural transaction uniqueness and replay prevention that comes from mutation of gas coin objects.
By bounding expiration and providing a nonce, validators must only retain executed digests for the maximum possible expiry range to differentiate retries from unique transactions with otherwise identical inputs.

member.summary: Upgrades a Move package. Takes (in order): 1. A vector of serialized modules for the package. 2. A vector of object ids for the transitive dependencies of the new package. 3. member.name: P:Sui.Rpc.V2.ChangeEpoch.SystemPackages
member.summary: System packages (specifically framework and Move stdlib) that are written before the new epoch starts. This tracks framework upgrades on chain. When executing the `ChangeEpoch` txn, the validator must write out the following modules. Modules are provided with the version they will be upgraded to, their modules in serialized form (which include their package ID), and a list of their transitive dependencies.
